name: GM P01_512Kb_LS1_LS6 OS12212156 - 2002 512kB PCM - v6 - no password

parameters.name: Matrix of multipliers for calculating cylinder airmass vs. engine op. index
parameters.description:
Matrix used for derivation of future airmass used for calculating the needed cylinder
fueling. The future airmass terms are a blend of the previous airmass, steady state
airmass calculated from spee

parameters.name: Oil Pressure Low Warning Threshold
parameters.description: The PCM will flag a low oil pressure condition if the actual oil pressure is below these values for the time defined in {Oil Pressure Low Warning Delay}.

parameters.name: Oil Pressure Gauge PWM Output Calibration
parameters.description:
This table determines the pulse width modulation for an oil pressure gauge that is driven directly from the PCM.

Enabled only on Vehicle Platforms (unless patched):
3  = GMT 530 (ex. GMC Kodiac/To

parameters.name: B3201 - Key-on Initial Fuel Prime Pulse
parameters.description:
When the ignition key is first turned to the on position the PCM will deliver a prime pulse of fuel.
This table defines the amount of fuel (in Grams) to be injected.

parameters.name: B3202 - Key-on Initial Fuel Prime Delay
parameters.description: Before the {B3201} "Initial Fuel Prime" pulse is delivered, the PCM will delay the prime pulse for this long after key on.

parameters.name: B3203 - Key-on Initial Fuel Prime Timer
parameters.description: The key on fuel prime pulse will only be delivered if the engine has been running for this amount of time since the previous key on fuel prime pulse was delivered. Prevents the prime pulse from being

parameters.name: Key-on Initial Fuel Prime Baro Correction
parameters.description: Baro correction multiplier applied to key-on fuel mass.

parameters.name: Key-on Initial Fuel Prime Pulse Heat Soak Correction
parameters.description: Used to compensate for heat soak from previous engine run time.

parameters.name: Non Sequential Fuel Pulse
parameters.description:
Before the PCM switches to sequential injection mode after starting this is the mass of fuel that will be injected.

B3204, B3206

parameters.name: Non Sequential Fuel Pulse Delay
parameters.description:
This many reference pulses must occur before the PCM will deliver the {B3204 and B3206} "Non Sequential Fuel Pulse".

There are 24 reference pulses per crankshaft revolution.

B3205, B3207

parameters.name: Non Sequential Fuel Pulse Heat Soak Correction
parameters.description: Used to compensate fuel prime pulses for heat soak from previous engine run time.

parameters.name: B3604 - Commanded Fuel When Cranking
parameters.description:
When the engine is cranking, this is the commanded fuel.
Based on engine coolant temperature at startup and the number of times the 24x sensor indicates 90° of engine rotation.

parameters.name: Commanded Fuel For Extended Cranking
parameters.description: Defines the desired inverse lambda when the engine is cranking for a prolonged period of time. Based on reference pulses recieved since prolonged cranking entered. Crank pulses cycle from 0-16 for as

parameters.name: Cranking Enleanment Factor
parameters.description: This table leans out the cranking fuel based in relation to ECT and RPM. It is a value between 0 and 1 and multiplies the Commanded Cranking Fuel tables.

parameters.name: B3627 - After-start Enrichment Decay Delay
parameters.description: Wait this many engine revolutions before leaning out the after-start enrichment.

parameters.name: B3628 - After-start Enrichment Decay Rate
parameters.description:
The after-start enrichment is decayed by {B3650} "After-start Enrichment Decay Factor" at this rate.
Higher values will cause a slower decay rate. Lower values will cause a faster decay rate.

parameters.name: B3629 - Park to Drive Enrichment
parameters.description: Enriches mixture for first shift from park into drive.

parameters.name: B3629 - Park to Drive Enrichment Delay
parameters.description: Delay before enabling Park to Drive Enrichment.

parameters.name: B3630 - Park to Drive Enrichment Decay Delay
parameters.description: Upon detecting a park/neutral to drive gear selection, wait this many engine revolutions before leaning out the park/neutral to drive enrichment.

parameters.name: B3631 - Park to Drive Enrichment Decay Rate
parameters.description: Defines the reference pulse period for decaying out intial park to drive enrichment.

parameters.name: B3632 - After-start Coolant Temp Enrichment
parameters.description: Inverse lambda added for enriching engine operation after startup based on coolant temp.

parameters.name: B3633 - After-start Intake Temp Enrichment
parameters.description: Inverse lambda added for enriching engine operation after startup based on intake temp.

parameters.name: B3639 - Lean Cruise Enleanment
parameters.description: Subtracted from current inverse lambda to provide enleanment for cruising conditions.

parameters.name: B3646 - Lean Cruise Coolant Temp Multiplier
parameters.description: Corrects lean cruise engine operation based on changes in coolant temp.

parameters.name: B3650 - After-start Enrichment Decay Factor
parameters.description:
The after-start enrichment is decayed by this factor for each {B3628} "After-start Enrichment Decay Rate" engine revolutions.
Values closer to 1 will cause a slower decay of the enrichment.
Values c

parameters.name: B3651 - Park to Drive Enrichment Decay Factor
parameters.description:
The park/neutral to drive enrichment is decayed by this factor for each {B3631} "Park to Drive Enrichment Decay Rate" engine revolutions.
Values closer to 1 will cause a slower decay of the enrichmen

parameters.name: B3661 - Restart Enrichment
parameters.description: This is the amount of additional enrichment that will be added on a restart based on the time between engine off and the re-start.

parameters.name: B3662 - After-start Enrichment Factor
parameters.description: Based on the time between engine off and the re-start this table alters the afterstart enrichment amount.

parameters.name: B3663 - Additional After-start Enrichment
parameters.description: Enrichment added on initial start-up to improve engine operation when engine is still cold.

parameters.name: B3664 - Additional After-start Enrichment Delay
parameters.description: This table defines the delay after the engine transitions from cranking to run mode before the additional aferstart fueling is added.

parameters.name: B3665 - Additional After-start Enrichment Active
parameters.description: This table defines how long the additional aferstart fueling is added.

parameters.name: B3701 - Injector Pulse Width Voltage Adjustment
parameters.description: As battery voltage and MAP varies, the injector pulse widths are adjusted by this much to compensate for the changes in the opening and closing times.

parameters.name: - Injector Pulse Width Vacuum Compensation
parameters.description: Compensates for effects of vacuum on residual exposed fuel.

parameters.name: B3702 - Target for End of Fuel Injection Pulse
parameters.description: Defines the desired point at which the fuel injection pulse will be completed by. The value is the position for the current cylinders next firing pulse in degrees after the current cylinders last refe

parameters.name: Target for End of Fuel Injection Pulse when Cranking
parameters.description: Defines the desired point at which the fuel injection pulse will be completed by. The value is the position for the current cylinders next firing pulse in degrees after the current cylinders last refe

parameters.name: B3703 - Target for End of Fuel Injection Pulse Trim
parameters.description:
This table modifies the timing of the additional pulses that maybe required after the {B3702} Injection Timing offset limits are reached.
These values should not exceed those in {B3702} Injection Tim

parameters.name: B3704 - Injector Bank Assignment
parameters.description:
Defines which cylinder channel is associated with each cylinder bank. Cylinder channels are from 0-7. 0 = cylinder bank #1, 1 = cylinder bank #2.
Example, SBC firing order is 1 8 4 3 6 5 7 2 will hav

parameters.name: Injector channel pulsewidth sampling enable vs. current cylinder channel
parameters.description:
Defines which cylinder channels can sample the 
current pulsewidth being delivered for the cylinder
in the firing order.

1 = allow sampling

parameters.name: B4001 - Injector Flow Rate
parameters.description:
Flow rate of the fuel injectors.
If the fuel rail pressure is NOT referenced to manifold pressure, then decreasing the manifold pressure has the same effect as increasing the fuel rail pressure. The

parameters.name: B4001 - Injector Flow Rate Boost
parameters.description:
Flow rate of the fuel injectors.
If the fuel rail pressure is NOT referenced to manifold pressure, then decreasing the manifold pressure has the same effect as increasing the fuel rail pressure. The

parameters.name: B4002 - Injector Fuel Pump Voltage Correction
parameters.description: Injector flow compensation based on fuel pump voltage.

parameters.name: B4003 - Injector Minimum Pulse Width
parameters.description: This is the minimum injector pulse width the PCM can command for a given RPM.

parameters.name: B4004 - Injector Default Minimum Pulse Width
parameters.description: If the PCM commands a injector pulse with less than {B4003} "Minimum Injector Pulse Width" then the injector pulse times from this table will be used instead.

parameters.name: B4005 - Injector Small Pulse Adjustment
parameters.description: When a very short injector on time is commanded (between 0 - 4mS) the PCM will add the values in this table onto the injector on time.

parameters.name: Fuel Gauge PWM Output Calibration
parameters.description:
This table determines the pulse width modulation for a fuel gauge that is driven directly from the PCM.
If this table is zeroed in your stock tune then assume the PCM does not control the fuel gauge

parameters.name: Fuel Sender Out of Range Time
parameters.description: Time for fuel sender to be above upper or below lower limit before fault is flagged.

parameters.name: F0507 - Convert Primary Sender to Volume
parameters.description: The PCM uses this table to convert the signal from the primary fuel tank level sender to actual liters for the PCM's internal calculations.

parameters.name: F0509 - Convert Secondary Sender to Volume
parameters.description: The PCM uses this table to convert the signal from the secondary fuel tank level sender to actual liters for the PCM's internal calculations.

parameters.name: Engine RPM for engine to be cranking
parameters.description: Engine RPMs must be above this for engine to be considered to be cranking.

parameters.name: # of reference pulses to transition from crank to run
B3603 - Hot Enrichment Mode Commanded Fuel
parameters.description:
When the PCM is in engine protection mode, this is the commanded fuel.
Engine Protection fuelling mode is enabled once the engine gets too hot.

parameters.name: B3605 - Open Loop Commanded Fuel
parameters.description: Fuel commanded during open loop conditions. Defined based on MAP and coolant temperature.

parameters.name: B3609 - PE Delay Counter Adjustment
parameters.description: Used to adjust how fast power enrichment mode becomes active at different RPMs.

parameters.name: B3615 - PE Hot Mode Enable
parameters.description: If the engine coolant temperature is above the hot engine threshold, then these throttle positions will be used to enable power enrichment.

parameters.name: B3616 - PE Normal Mode Enable
parameters.description: If the engine coolant temperature is below the hot engine threshold, then these throttle positions will be used to enable power enrichment.

parameters.name: B3617 - PE Modifier Based on Coolant Temp
parameters.description: Inverse Lambda added to power enrich lambda to compensate power enrichment for engine temps.

parameters.name: B3618 - PE Commanded Fuel
parameters.description: When in power enrichment mode the commanded air to fuel ratio will be defined by this table.

parameters.name: - PE Minimum RPM Threshold vs Baro
B3641 - PE Modifier Based on Intake Temp
parameters.description: Inverse Lambda added to power enrich lambda to compensate power enrichment for intake temps.

parameters.name: B3659 - Piston Protection Commanded Fuel
parameters.description:
This is the commanded fuel once the PCM enters Piston Protection mode.
Piston Protection mode will become active under sustained heavy load conditions.

parameters.name: B3406 - Impact Factor
parameters.description: Defines the fraction of fuel that will remain on the intake port wall per injection.

parameters.name: B3401 - Evaporation Time
parameters.description: Defines how long it will take for the impacted fuel to evaporate off the intake port wall.

parameters.name: Reference pulse threshold to provide compensation for intake port wall wetting
B3426 - Intial decay multiplier for throttle opening compensation fuel mass
parameters.description: Initial decay multiplier for decaying out fuel mass provided for throttle opening. Only used at engine start-up.

parameters.name: Throttle Opening Compensation Fuel Mass Decay Rate Multiplier
parameters.description: Used to decay out fuel mass for throttle opening.

parameters.name: Cylinder channel in firing order to recieve dynamic fuel mass compensation
parameters.description:
Defines the cylinder channel that will have the calculated dynamic fuel mass using this current channel applied to it. The cylinder channel refers to the PCMs driver output. 
Example: Channel # 0-1-2

parameters.name: Cylinder in firing order to use for constructing dynamic fuel mass
parameters.description: Example: Channel # 0-1-2-3-4-5-6-7 corresponds to cylinders 1-8-4-3-6-5-7-2 on a SBC.

parameters.name: B3428 - Gain multiplier for previous throttle opening fuel mass
parameters.description: Used for adding in a portion of the previous fuel mass to the upcoming fuel mass for compensating for sudden throttle opening. Actual fuel mass based on the rate that the cylinder fuelmass is changing

parameters.name: Multiplier for compensating throttle opening compensation fuel mass
parameters.description: Applies corrections for the ammount of heat retained by the engine after shut-down.

parameters.name: Impact Factor Airflow Multiplier
parameters.description: Adjusts the impact factor constant for fuel that wets the intake port walls based on ammount of airflow into the engine.

parameters.name: Evaporation Time Airflow Multiplier
parameters.description: Compensates intake port wall fuel evaporation time constant for changes in airflow.

parameters.name: B4512 - Airflow Derivative Correction
parameters.description:
Airflow correction to be applied based on RPM rate of change which tries to dampen overshoot of desired RPM.
If RPM is increasing the value in this calibration is subtracted from the desired airflow,

parameters.name: B4514 - Airflow Integral Correction
parameters.description:
Correction to learned airflow when engine speed is too high or too low and transmission is in drive or Park/Neutral.

Absolute RPM error is the difference between current engine speed and desired en

parameters.name: B4515 - Airflow Proportional Correction
parameters.description:
Correction to direct airflow when engine speed is too high or too low under various transmission and A/C conditions.

Absolute RPM error is the difference between current engine speed and desired en

parameters.name: B4403 - IAC Effective Area
parameters.description: Used to convert effective area (which the PCM calculates from the desired idle airflow) into actual IAC motor steps.

parameters.name: B4603 - Desired Engine Idle Speeds
parameters.description:
Defines the desired idle speed that will be targeted when closed loop idle is in effect.

Columns are in order:
1. In Gear, A/C Off,
2. In Gear, A/C On,
3. Park/Neutral, A/C Off,
4. Park/Neutral

parameters.name: B4604 - Startup RPM Offset
parameters.description:
Upon engine startup this is the RPM offset added to the values in 
  - {B4603} Desired Idle Speeds.

See Also: 
  - {B4606} Startup Decay Delay in Gear
  - {B4607} Startup Decay Delay in P/N
  -

parameters.name: B4605 - Heater Warmup RPM Offset
parameters.description:
In an attempt to get the engine hotter faster and increase the heater temperature this is the RPM offset added to the values in 
  - {B4603} Desired Idle Speeds.

See Also: 
  - {B4610} Heater War

parameters.name: B4521 - Max Idle Short Term Correction
parameters.description:
This is the maximum correction the short term idle learn cells can adjust to.
Defines the max difference between the A/C on/off and P/N, drive cells to prevent the airflow in the cells from differing

parameters.name: B4522 - Stall Saver Activation Thresholds
parameters.description: RPM threshold below which the stall saver logic will be activated. The threshold is based on the desired idle RPM. When a stall is impending, the derivative corrections are reset to the current refere

parameters.name: B4304 - Startup Airflow Correction
parameters.description:
Correction to airflow on startup. This value is added with {B4343} "Startup Friction Airflow Correction" for a final airflow correction value on startup.

See Also: 
  - {B4340} Startup Airflow Dec

parameters.name: B4307 - Desired Airflow
parameters.description:
Used to reduce airflow as the engine warms up.
Although listed in the "Idle" section, this calibration also controls the base airflow for non-idle conditions.

parameters.name: B4308 - Airflow Parked
parameters.description:
When the IAC motor is in the park position this is the required airflow.
The IAC will be moved to the park position in situations like engine shutdown and key on.

NOTE: Remember this table is base

parameters.name: B4309 - Throttle Cracker Airflow in Gear
parameters.description:
Airflow values added to idle airflow when the throttle cracker function is active and the transmission is in gear.

If both the throttle cracker airflow and the throttle follower airflow are non zer

parameters.name: B4310 - Throttle Cracker Airflow in P/N
parameters.description:
Airflow values added to idle airflow when the throttle cracker function is active and the transmission is in neutral or the clutch is pressed.

If both the throttle cracker airflow and the throttle

parameters.name: B4313 - Throttle Cracker Decay Rate
parameters.description:
When the throttle cracker is deactivated, the throttle cracker airflow value is decayed by this amount every 100ms.

See also: 
  - {B4312} Throttle Cracker Deactivate Speed

parameters.name: B4314 - Throttle Cracker Decay Delay
parameters.description:
When the throttle cracker is to be deactivated, the PCM will wait this long before doing so.

See also: 
  - {B4312} Throttle Cracker Deactivate Speed

parameters.name: B4315 - Throttle Follower Airflow
parameters.description:
Airflow values added to idle airflow when the throttle follower function is active.
This value is multiplied by {B4316} "Throttle Follower Airflow RPM Multiplier" to obtain the final throttle followe

parameters.name: B4316 - Throttle Follower Airflow RPM Multiplier
parameters.description:
The airflow values from {B4315} "Throttle Follower Airflow" are multiplied by this value to obtain the final throttle follower airflow value.

If both the throttle cracker airflow and the throttle f

parameters.name: Throttle Follower Airflow Adder For Auto Trans Manual Downshift
parameters.description: Additional throttle follower airflow applied when automatic transmission is present and manual downshift is commanded.

parameters.name: B4317 - Throttle Follower Decay Rate in Gear
parameters.description:
When the transmission is in gear, the throttle follower airflow value is decayed by this amount every 12.5ms.

See also: 
  - {B4321} Throttle Follower TPS change

parameters.name: B4318 - Throttle Follower Decay Delay in Gear
parameters.description:
When the throttle follower airflow is to be decayed and the transmission is in gear, the PCM will wait this long before starting the decay.

See also: 
  - {B4321} Throttle Follower TPS change

parameters.name: B4319 - Throttle Follower Decay Rate in P/N
parameters.description:
When the transmission is in neutral or the clutch is pressed, the throttle follower airflow value is decayed by this amount every 12.5ms.

See also: 
  - {B4321} Throttle Follower TPS change

parameters.name: B4320 - Throttle Follower Decay Delay in P/N
parameters.description:
When the throttle follower airflow is to be decayed and the transmission is in neutral or the clutch is pressed, the PCM will wait this long before starting the decay.

See also: 
  - {B4321} Throt

parameters.name: IAC Resync Steps
parameters.description: IAC steps change during IAC resynchronization depending on measured idle speed error.

parameters.name: B4332 - Rolling Desired Idle Speed
parameters.description:
The desired idle speed when the vehicle is not stationary and the automatic transmission is in neutral or the clutch is depressed.
Rolling desired idle mode may only be active if the throttle cracker

parameters.name: B4336 - Rolling Desired Idle High Correction
parameters.description:
Rolling Desired Idle airflow correction when rpm is too high.

See also: 
  - {B4333} Rolling Desired Idle Disable Speed
  - {B4334} Rolling Desired Idle Enable Speed
  - {B4335} Rolling Desired

parameters.name: B4337 - Rolling Desired Idle Low Correction
parameters.description:
Rolling Desired Idle airflow correction when rpm is too low.

See also: 
  - {B4333} Rolling Desired Idle Disable Speed
  - {B4334} Rolling Desired Idle Enable Speed
  - {B4335} Rolling Desired I

parameters.name: B4338 - Engine Speed Compensation
parameters.description:
Extra airflow required to compensate for the load placed on the engine by external loads such as:
- Initial startup
- Alternator
- Heater

This value is multiplied by {B4339} "Engine Speed Compen

parameters.name: B4339 - Engine Speed Compensation Multiplier
parameters.description:
Multiplier for {B4338} "Engine Speed Compensation".
May be used to modify the engine speed compensation at various engine coolant temperatures.

parameters.name: B4342 - Startup Airflow Delay
parameters.description:
Startup idle airflow correction begins to decay after this many camshaft revolutions.

See also: 
  - {B4304} Startup Airflow Correction
  - {B4340} Startup Airflow Decay

parameters.name: B4343 - Startup Friction Airflow Correction
parameters.description:
Correction to idle airflow on startup to overcome initial starting friction and cold/stiff engine components.
This value is added to {B4304} "Startup Airflow Correction" for a final airflow correctio

parameters.name: B4344 - Startup Friction Airflow Decay
parameters.description:
Startup friction airflow correction is decayed by this amount, every 90 degrees of crankshaft rotation.

See also: 
  - {B4343} Startup Friction Airflow Correction
  - {B4345} Startup Friction Air

parameters.name: B4345 - Startup Friction Airflow Delay
parameters.description:
Startup friction airflow correction begins to decay after this many camshaft revolutions.

See also: 
  - {B4343} Startup Friction Airflow Correction
  - {B4344} Startup Friction Airflow Decay

parameters.name: B3303 - Fuel Cut-off Limiters in Gear
parameters.description: These calibrations define various fuel cut-off stages, when transmission is in gear.

parameters.name: B3305 - Cold Engine Rev Protection Timer
parameters.description: The PCM reads the engine coolant temperature at startup, then based on that temperature, this table determines how long the cold engine RPM limiters will be used.

parameters.name: B3306 - Cold Engine Rev Limiter
parameters.description: If the PCM has determined the engine is in a cold condition, based on engine coolant temperature at startup, this is the RPM limiter table that will be used.

parameters.name: B5401 - ETC Controlled RPM Limit
parameters.description: RPM limits when RPM limiting is performed by the Electronic Throttle and not Fuel Cutoff.

parameters.name: B6202 - Knock Retard Fast Attack Gain
parameters.description:
This table sets the gain or adjustment range of the knock system, dependent on engine coolant temperature (ECT).
Example, to allow full knock retard adjustments, set to 1 and to disable, set to 0.

parameters.name: B6203 - Knock Retard Fast Attack Rate
parameters.description:
Once knock is detected, the timing will be reduced by this many degrees per knock sensor volt, every low resolution reference pulse (4x per crank rotation for V8, 3x for V6).
Total spark retard is ba

parameters.name: B6204 - Knock Retard Limit Not in PE Mode
parameters.description: Defines max allowed knock retard for normal engine operation (not in PE mode).

parameters.name: B6205 - Knock Retard Limit When in PE Mode
parameters.description: Defines the max allowed knock retard when engine is in Power Enrichment mode.

parameters.name: B6206 - Knock Retard Default Value with Knock DTC
parameters.description: If there is a fault with the knock control system, then this amount of timing will be retarded, regardless.

parameters.name: B6207 - Knock Retard Recovery Rate
parameters.description: If timing has been retarded due to knock, but knock has stopped, then the amount of retard will be reduced by this amount, every low resolution reference pulse (4x per crank rotation for V8, 3x for V6

parameters.name: Knock Retard Baro Based Gain
parameters.description: Baro based gain used to generate spark retard based on ammount of detected knock.

parameters.name: B6210 - Burst Knock Retard Delta Airmass
parameters.description: If cylinder airmass changes more than this, PCM will apply burst knock retard

parameters.name: B6212 - Burst Knock Retard Spark Retard
parameters.description: This ammount of retard will be applied when burst knock retard becomes active.

parameters.name: B6226 - Knock Sensor Frequency
parameters.description:
The 'center' frequency for knock sensors.
Any signals picked up by the knock sensor at these frequencies will be processed by the PCM.

Only the following values may be entered into this table:
0

parameters.name: B6227 - Knock Sensor Filter
parameters.description:
The noise filter for the knock sensors.
This provides further filtering of noise from the knock sensor.

Only the following values may be entered into this table:
0 = 1.65
1 = 2.50
2 = 4.70
3 =

parameters.name: B6228 - Knock Sensor Sensitivity
parameters.description:
This table defines the amplification characteristics of the knock sensors.

Only the following values may be entered into this table:
0 = 0.0 dB
1 = 2.0 dB
2 = 4.0 dB
3 = 6.0 dB
4 = 8.0 dB
5 =

parameters.name: B6244 - Knock Sensor Multiplier A (Cylinder Mode)
parameters.description:
This multiplier is applied when not in RPM Knock Mode or TPS Knock Mode, otherwise the appropriate RPM or TPS knock multipliers are applied.
This value is multiplied with the learned knock threshold

parameters.name: B6245 - Knock Sensor Multiplier B (Cylinder Mode)
parameters.description:
This multiplier is always applied when knock detection is in Cylinder Mode.

Higher values desensitize the knock sensors.
Lower values sensitize the knock sensors.

parameters.name: B6246 - Knock Sensor Multiplier A (Engine Mode)
parameters.description:
This multiplier is applied when not in RPM Knock Mode or TPS Knock Mode, otherwise the appropriate RPM or TPS knock multipliers are applied.
This value is multiplied with the learned knock threshold

parameters.name: B6247 - Knock Sensor Multiplier B (Engine Mode)
parameters.description:
This multiplier is always applied when knock detection is in Engine Mode.

Higher values desensitize the knock sensors.
Lower values sensitize the knock sensors.

parameters.name: B6250 - Knock Sensor Multiplier TPS
parameters.description:
This multiplier is applied when in TPS Knock Mode.
TPS Knock Mode is active when all three TPS conditions are met: Minimum, Maximum and Delta.

This value is multiplied with the learned knock thres

parameters.name: B6255 - Knock Sensor Multiplier RPM
parameters.description:
This multiplier is applied when in RPM Knock Mode.
RPM Knock Mode is active when all three RPM conditions are met: Minimum, Maximum and Delta.

This value is multiplied with the learned knock thres

parameters.name: B5934 - After start engine idle flare speed control spark advance
parameters.description: Used to dampen out idle flare after engine starts up.

parameters.name: Max allowed rate to change spark advance during low RPMs
Cool temp multiplier for afterstart idle flare control spark advance
parameters.description: Compensates SA for controlling idle spark flare control based on cool temp.

parameters.name: B5935 - Idle speed control spark advance for idle overspeed
parameters.description: Used to control engine idle speed fluctuations.

parameters.name: B5936 - Idle speed control spark advance for idle underspeed
parameters.description: Used to control engine idle speed during idle underspeed.

parameters.name: C2911 - MAF Rationality Test
parameters.description: This table defines the maximum difference that is allowed between the calculated airflow (using MAP) and actual measured airflow (MAF).
